description_long: This object is a large, cylindrical wooden water tower elevated on four angled wooden legs, with a circular metal platform at the top and a diamond-plate patterned metal lid. The wood appears weathered and whitewashed, giving it a rustic, aged look, while the metal lid is slightly domed and reflective. A wooden ladder is attached to one side, providing access to the top, and the structure is reinforced with a wooden ring around the base of the tank. The tank itself is constructed from vertical and horizontal wooden planks, with visible seams and slight color variations. The overall design is utilitarian, suggesting it is meant for outdoor use, likely in an urban or industrial setting. There are no visible handles, labels, or markings on the exterior.
description: A large, weathered wooden water tower with a metal lid, elevated on four angled legs and featuring a side ladder. The structure is rustic and utilitarian, suitable for outdoor urban or industrial environments.
description_view_0: The front view shows the cylindrical tank with vertical wooden planks, the metal lid, and two of the four supporting legs clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the attached wooden ladder leading up to the tank, as well as the circular metal lid and the full ring reinforcement at the tank's base.
description_view_2: The side view displays the horizontal wooden planks of the tank, the domed metal lid, and the ladder partially visible to the left.
description_view_3: This rear view shows the back side of the tank with vertical planks and the supporting legs, with no ladder visible.
